items.prompt: Which principle does subgroup performance auditing before clinical deployment best illustrate?
items.explain: Example 3.2 ties non-maleficence to preventing discriminatory harm from skewed training data.

items.prompt: Example 3.3 (hiring algorithm) primarily illustrates which principle?
items.explain: A ranker reproducing historical hiring patterns is a fairness risk, not merely a accuracy issue.

items.prompt: Transparency requires only publishing a privacy policy with no explanation of consequential decisions.
items.explain: Transparency includes disclosing collection purposes and, where feasible, how consequential automated decisions are produced.

items.prompt: Example 3.6 (health-tracking app) best matches which principle?
items.explain: Explicit consent, plain purposes, and revocable permissions operationalize autonomy.

items.prompt: List two of the five core principles from Table 3.2.
items.rubric: Any two from: non-maleficence, fairness, transparency, accountability, respect for autonomy.
